holdings.note: SpaceX — IPO'd Jun 2026; FMP may not yet cover earnings/dividend dates for a brand-new listing.

macro.label: Triple witching
macro.detail: Quarterly simultaneous expiry of index futures, index options & single-stock options — volume/volatility spike and pinning risk in the most options-heavy names.

INTC.read: INTC is down 6.2% today on below-average volume with no clean news catalyst, and there are no dated company events on the calendar — no confirmed earnings, no ex-div in the window provided. With the 5-day still green (+3.44%), today reads more like a one-day positioning flush than a trend break, but without a dated catalyst to anchor an add or trim, the right posture is to wait for either a confirmed earnings date or a clear reversal/follow-through before committing.
INTC.bull: Volume-light 6% drop with a still-positive 5-day suggests today is noise, not a thesis break.
INTC.bear: No dated company catalyst means no near-term event to force a re-rate if selling pressure continues.

NVDA.read: NVDA is down 4% on the day and ~6% on the week with no company-specific dated catalyst on the board — this is a tape/positioning move, not an event. With only triple witching ~86 days out as macro and no confirmed earnings date in hand, there's no defined window to lean into yet; the right move is to let price establish a base or wait for the next earnings date to print before sizing a decision.
NVDA.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (macro only; no company catalyst on calendar)
NVDA.bull: Quality compounder pulling back into a vacuum of news — weakness without a binary event is usually the better add zone once price stabilizes.
NVDA.bear: No dated catalyst means no clear floor; a 6% weekly drawdown can extend if it's the front edge of a positioning unwind or AI-capex rerate.

ORCL.read: ORCL is down 14% over five days with no dated company catalyst on the calendar — the selling pressure is real (RSI 35, heavy volume) but the driver isn't in the supplied feed, which makes "add the dip" premature without knowing what's being discounted. With the next earnings print not yet on the schedule here and only triple witching ~86 days out as a macro marker, the disciplined move is to wait for either a confirmed catalyst date or a stabilization signal before committing fresh capital.
ORCL.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (macro only; no dated ORCL company catalyst supplied)
ORCL.bull: Oversold quality name on heavy volume with no confirmed fundamental driver — reversion setup if the unidentified overhang clears.
ORCL.bear: A 14% five-day drawdown without a visible catalyst usually means someone knows something; knife-catching before the news surfaces is how good positions become bad ones.

NOK.read: NOK is down 5.1% on the day and 7.6% on the week with no company-specific catalyst on the calendar — no confirmed earnings date, no ex-div, nothing dated to anchor an add window. Without a known event to fade into or position for, this is a price-action read only: you want to see selling exhaust and a base form before adding, or wait for the next earnings date to be confirmed. Triple witching on 2026-09-18 is macro noise, not a NOK-specific driver.
NOK.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (macro only; no NOK-specific dated catalyst on file)
NOK.bull: Sharp two-week drawdown in a low-multiple name with no bad news pinned to it sets up a mean-reversion window once a catalyst gets dated.
NOK.bear: No dated catalyst means no defined floor — the -7.6% week can extend, and you're catching knives without knowing what's driving the tape.

NBIS.read: No company-specific dated catalyst is on the sheet for NBIS — no confirmed earnings, no ex-div — so there's no real window to lean into yet. With only a tiny 1-share position and the next visible event being triple witching on 2026-09-18 (a flow event, not a fundamental one), this is a name to keep on the radar until an earnings date prints. The actionable moment comes when Nebius confirms its next quarterly print or drops a capacity/AI-cloud customer update.
NBIS.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (macro flow event; no company catalyst on calendar)
NBIS.bull: AI-infrastructure buildout narrative and GPU capacity ramp can re-rate the name on any confirmed customer or revenue milestone.
NBIS.bear: High-beta AI-cloud exposure with no near-term dated catalyst means price is at the mercy of sentiment and rates until earnings is confirmed.

KO.read: No company-specific dated catalyst on the board for KO right now — no confirmed earnings or ex-div in the feed — and the tape is doing nothing dramatic (+0.96% day, -0.75% week) at $80.32. With a 5-share position and no binary event to lean into, there's no edge in acting; the next real window opens when an earnings date or ex-div lands on the calendar.
KO.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (macro only; no KO-specific catalyst posted)
KO.bull: Defensive cash-flow compounder drifting quietly while we wait for the next dated print or dividend declaration to define a window.
KO.bear: Without a confirmed catalyst, the position just absorbs tape risk and macro events like triple witching with no scheduled re-rating event to lean on.

SPCX.read: SPCX is a fresh June 2026 IPO and the data vendor hasn't backfilled earnings or dividend dates yet, so there's no company-confirmed catalyst to anchor an add/trim window. With only 10 shares this is a starter/tracker position — the near-term tape will be driven by lockup mechanics and the first post-IPO print, neither of which are dated here. Triple witching on 9/18 is the only scheduled item and it's a flow event, not a fundamental one, so the honest move is to wait for the first earnings date to surface.
SPCX.next_catalyst: 2026-09-18 — Triple witching (only dated event on the calendar; no SPCX-specific catalyst confirmed yet)
SPCX.bull: Scarcity bid on a unique pure-play SpaceX listing; first earnings could re-rate the name once Street models exist.
SPCX.bear: Post-IPO lockup expiry and absence of a confirmed earnings date leave the position exposed to supply shocks with no fundamental catalyst to defend it.

disclaimer: Forward earnings dates are third-party aggregator estimates, not company-confirmed — confirm against the company's IR release (~2 weeks ahead). Dividends, macro prints and witching are scheduled/confirmed. Not investment advice.
